retrieved_docs.title: Histology, Goblet Cells -- Clinical Significance
retrieved_docs.content: Chronic infections can cause goblet cell depletion and thus cause immunologic implications; goblet cell secretion of mucus encourages pathogen elimination and safeguards the protective mucus layers. Besides chronic infections, parasitic intestinal infections can significantly affect these cells. Some studies have also implicated goblet cells to play a role in cystic fibrosis. Two major inflammatory bowel diseases that may involve goblet cells are Crohn disease and ulcerative colitis. In ulcerative colitis, goblet cell number and size drastically decrease. Mucin, the main product of goblet cells, plays an essential role in maintaining protective mucus barriers, and the breakdown of these barriers is thought to result in colitis eventually. [12] Pools of superfluous amounts of extracellular mucin characterize mucinous carcinoma. Mucinous carcinoma is a tumor where 50% of the tumor mass comes from mucin. This carcinoma leads to high levels of MUC2 goblet cell mucin. MUC2 becomes upregulated and, thus, increases the binding of the goblet cell lineage transcription factor to the MUC2 promoter. [12]

retrieved_docs.title: Autosomal Dominant Tubulointerstitial Kidney Disease -- Pathophysiology
retrieved_docs.content: In autosomal dominant tubulointerstitial kidney disease-MUC1, the MUC1 gene is located on chromosome 1q21. It is thought that most MUC1 mutations are caused by cytosine duplication within a seven-cytosine stretch in the variable-number tandem repeats (VNTR) region that usually produces a frameshift mutation. This mutated gene produces an abnormal protein known as the MUC1 frameshift protein (MUC1fs). Accumulating this abnormal protein (MUC1fs) in the renal tubular epithelial cells may be considered an important pathogenic factor in the pathophysiology of ADTKD-MUC1; in addition, positive staining of (MUC1fs) in the urinary exfoliated cells and renal tubular epithelial cells could help to diagnose this condition. [1]

retrieved_docs.title: Rheumatoid Arthritis -- Complications -- Complications and Comorbidities Associated with Uncontrolled Systemic Inflammation
retrieved_docs.content: Pleuritis, bronchiolitis, and interstitial lung disease are also associated with RA. Although rare, RA treatment with methotrexate and anti-TNF agents can also lead to pulmonary injury. There is also an increased risk of pulmonary embolism in RA. Interstitial lung disease (ILD) affects 5 to 16% of RA patients and is associated with RA-specific autoantibodies and increased mortality. [66] [67] [68] Some patients may present with ILD before developing joint inflammation with positive RF and ACPA. RA-associated ILD is associated with ACPA, anti-carbamylated protein antibodies (anti-CarP), and anti-malondialdehyde-acetaldehyde antibodies (anti-MAA). [69] [70] [71] The MUC5B promoter variant rs35705950 is associated with ILD in patients with RA. This gene encodes mucin 5B, and this variant is a gain of function. RA-ILD compared to unaffected controls, the odds ratio is 3.8 (95% CI 2.8-5.2), and RA-ILD, compared to RA without ILD, the odds ratio is 3.1 (95% CI 1.8-5.4). [72]

retrieved_docs.title: Autosomal Dominant Tubulointerstitial Kidney Disease -- Etiology
retrieved_docs.content: Autosomal dominant tubulointerstitial kidney disease is a rare renal disorder caused by variable mutations in different genes. As discussed previously, at least five different gene mutations have been observed to result in this group of disorders. An autosomal dominant inheritance pattern inherits this disorder. MUC1 gene encodes a mucin-1 protein, UMOD gene encodes uromodulin protein (previously known as Tamm-Horsfall protein), HNF1B gene encodes hepatocyte nuclear factor 1 beta, REN gene encodes renin, and SEC61A1 gene encodes translocon subunit SEC61A. [5] The details of the pathogenesis underlying these genetic mutations will be discussed in the pathophysiology section of this article.

retrieved_docs.title: Atypical Glandular Cells (AGS) -- Biochemical and Genetic Pathology
retrieved_docs.content: Molecular analysis by microarray revealed the upregulation of CEACAM5, TACSTD1, S100P, and MSLN, with the expression of CEACAM5 and TACSTD1 associated with worse survival outcomes in endocervical and uterine adenocarcinoma. [17]

retrieved_docs.title: Atypical Glandular Cells (AGS) -- Causes
retrieved_docs.content: About 80% to 95% of endocervical adenocarcinomas are associated with HPV subtypes. Usual-type adenocarcinoma of the cervix is related to high-risk HPV subtypes like HPV-16 or HPV-18. HPV accounts for 50-58% of endocervical adenocarcinomas. Endocervical adenocarcinoma- mucinous,  gastric type is commonly seen in Japan and is associated with HIK1083 and MUC6 immune profile expression and germline inactivation of LKB1 seen in patients with Peutz-Jegher’s syndrome (also called adenoma malignant). Mucinous carcinoma, intestinal-type, is a rare type that shows 85% positivity to MUC-2 and is found to be associated with HPV. [4] In HPV-associated cancers, the risk of malignancy is dependent on the viral burden. [7]

retrieved_docs.title: Physiology, Cellular Receptors -- Pathophysiology -- Overexpression
retrieved_docs.content: Overexpression of cellular receptors occurs due to gene amplification, which increases the copy number of a receptor gene, or through overactivity of a gene promoter. This leads to upregulation of the signaling pathways associated with the receptor.

retrieved_docs.title: Tissue Evaluation for HER2 Tumor Marker -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: The human epidermal growth factor receptor-2 (HER2) receptor (previously called HER2/Neu) is a transmembrane glycoprotein with tyrosine kinase activity that belongs to the epidermal growth factor receptor family. These receptors are essential in controlling epithelial cell growth and differentiation. [1] These receptors are important, as aberrant HER2 protein overexpression has associations with some adenocarcinomas, including breast, ovary, endometrium, cervix, as well as lung, esophageal, gastroesophageal junction, gastric, and bladder cancers. [2] HER2 amplification or overexpression occurs in approximately 20% to 30% of human breast cancers. [3] This protein is strongly associated with increased disease recurrence and is a poor prognostic factor for survival. For this reason, HER2 is an essential target for the therapy of various types of cancer.

retrieved_docs.title: Mucoepidermoid Lung Tumor -- Pathophysiology
retrieved_docs.content: Because of its rarity and the small number of studies published, the precise nature and the histogenesis of mucoepidermoid carcinoma are still not well-known. Mucoepidermoid carcinomas originate from submucosal minor salivary-type glands in large airways. A fusion gene CRTC1-MAML2 occurs in both low-grade and high-grade mucoepidermoid carcinoma, [6] more frequently in low-grade tumors. [7] This fusion gene has potential as a diagnostic marker. Reports exist of EGFR mutations in some cases, but their significance is unclear. [8]

retrieved_docs.title: Mucinous Breast Carcinoma -- Histopathology -- Immunohistochemistry:
retrieved_docs.content: Typically, mucinous carcinoma is positive for estrogen and progesterone receptors [9] , while androgen receptors are expressed at a low level, and HER2 is not amplified [10] . Pure and mixed mucinous carcinomas are reported to express WT [11] . Mucinous breast carcinoma expresses predominantly MUC2 and MUC6 among the family of MUC genes [12] .

retrieved_docs.title: Histology, Goblet Cells -- Pathophysiology
retrieved_docs.content: Mucus hypersecretion due to goblet cell hyperplasia is a symptom of asthma and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease. Hyperplasia refers to areas where the excessive proliferation of goblet cells occurs in areas where they are normally present, such as large airways. As a result of this goblet cell proliferation, the usual protective role of goblet cell mucin secretion transforms into a pathophysiological role. The proliferation of goblet cells can also contribute to respiratory disease through changes in volume and biophysical features of airway mucus. [5]

retrieved_docs.title: Otitis Media With Effusion -- Pathophysiology -- Gastroesophageal Reflux and Allergy
retrieved_docs.content: Pepsins and Helicobacter pylori have been identified in middle ear effusions, suggesting a link to Gastroesophageal reflux. [37] [38] Children with chronic rhinitis, turbinate hypertrophy, asthma, or allergies may face an elevated risk. [39] Mucin gene activation may also contribute, with membrane-bound proteins, eg, MUC1, MUC3 , and MUC4 facilitating microbial attachment, while MUC5AC and MUC5B promote mucus accumulation in the middle ear. [40] [41]

retrieved_docs.title: Sputum Analysis -- Diagnostic Tests
retrieved_docs.content: Sputum molecular analysis is a novel and advanced technique used to detect lung cancer–related biomarkers, aiding in the early diagnosis of lung cancer. Many DNA mutations, including p53 ,  KRAS , EML4-ALK ,  and GFR , have been investigated in sputum specimens. [13] [14] [15] DNA hypermethylation has also been reported in sputum samples from patients with lung cancer. Loss of heterozygosity and microsatellite instability have been found in sputum specimens of patients with lung cancer using DNA markers. [16] MicroRNAs, such as miR-21 and miR-155, and proteins, such as a proliferation-inducing ligand (APRIL) and complement factor H, were significantly overexpressed. Some messenger RNAs, such as APRIL , MAGE , Telomerase , and CEA , have been found to rapidly degrade in the sputum specimens of patients with lung cancer, as detected by RT-PCR and immunocytochemistry. [17] Other molecular biomarkers, such as free and mitochondrial DNA variants, also appear promising. [17]

retrieved_docs.title: Renal Cystic Disease -- Pathophysiology
retrieved_docs.content: Autosomal dominant tubulointerstitial kidney disease is also characterized by progressive interstitial fibrosis and tubular damage due to ciliary dysfunction. Patients usually present with ESRD within 10 years of when their affected parent had ESRD. At least 6 separate genes are known to be involved, and the most common are UMOD , MUC1 , and REN . Other physiologic abnormalities, including polydipsia and polyuria, may also result. [33] [40] Nephronophthisis is an autosomal recessive genetic disorder caused by mutations in the NPH gene. There are 26 known mutations, and many more are unidentified.

retrieved_docs.title: Pseudomyxoma Peritonei -- Histopathology
retrieved_docs.content: Researchers have suggested MUC 2 over-expression as a molecular marker for PMP of intestinal origin. [19] Appendiceal tumors also express CK20, CEA, and CDX2, and are usually negative for CK7 and CA 125. [15] There are also reports of loss of protein expression of the repair genes MLH1 and PMS2. [20]

retrieved_docs.title: Autosomal Dominant Tubulointerstitial Kidney Disease -- Evaluation
retrieved_docs.content: In ADTKD- MUC1, genetic diagnosis is not straightforward. It is essential to know that testing for the MUC1 gene mutation is not a direct mutational analysis, and this test is not performed at commercial clinical laboratories at this time. A recent study was conducted to detect MUC1fs protein by immunostaining the MUC1fs protein on the epithelial tissues and the urinary cell smears in individuals with ADTKD- MUC1, and the result is promising. Still, it is not readily available at the clinical lab yet. [27]

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1795
retrieved_docs.content: The skin disorder حُمامَى is linked to other inflammatory conditions of the skin, indicating that it shares pathogenic mechanisms and clinical associations with additional inflammatory dermatoses. This “associated_with” relationship highlights the relevance of recognizing حُمامَى within the broader spectrum of skin inflammation for accurate diagnosis and comprehensive patient management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 18 -- Level 1 -- Community 81
retrieved_docs.content: congenital anomalies** contain hereditary and genetic disorders, which also comprise **other hereditary and degenerative nervous system conditions** and sexually transmitted infections; this hereditary‑genetic group is a member of both **فالج طفلي** and **متلازمة ستيكلر**. Collectively, the integrated structure demonstrates how endocrine, metabolic, fluid‑electrolyte, renal, infectious, vascular, otolaryngologic, behavioral, psychiatric, genetic, and developmental concepts intersect, providing a comprehensive framework for patient assessment, risk stratification, and multidisciplinary clinical management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 25 -- Level 4 -- Community 2888
retrieved_docs.content: Malignant Cellular Infiltrate is recognized as a diagnostic finding of cancer of the ovary, indicating the presence of abnormal tissue within the ovarian region. Similarly, Tumor cells, malignant are identified as the abnormal cellular component of cancer of the ovary, representing the malignant cells that drive the disease process. Together, these pathological entities underscore the cellular abnormalities that characterize ovarian cancer and are essential for its diagnosis and understanding.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 25 -- Level 4 -- Community 2796
retrieved_docs.content: Allowed qualifier is identified as a genetic factor (genetika) associated with several specific conditions, including اِضطِراب اللسان (tongue disorder), فَرْطُ الدَّرَقِيَّةِ (excessive tearing), and فَرْطُ ضَغْطِ الدَّم العيني (increased intra‑ocular pressure). Recognizing this genetic linkage can aid clinicians in understanding the hereditary contributions to these disorders and may inform diagnostic evaluation and personalized management strategies.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 24 -- Level 1 -- Community 257
retrieved_docs.content: importance of assessing metastatic potential even when the primary lesion’s benign versus malignant nature is unclear. Collectively, these interrelated entities form a hierarchical and functional network that connects genetic syndromes, primary gastrointestinal cancers, various metastatic malignancies, and skin melanomas, providing essential context for accurate diagnosis, staging, and management in patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 11 -- Level 2 -- Community 1082
retrieved_docs.content: Moreover, diagnostic observations and pathological findings related to the choroidea are specifically localized to the site termed “حَثَلُ المشيمية الوراثي,” indicating a genetically related placental structure. Precise identification of choroidal involvement at this site is essential for accurate diagnosis, management, and prognostication of the diverse ocular diseases that arise within the choroidea.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 30 -- Level 4 -- Community 2918
retrieved_docs.content: Malignant tumor cells represent the abnormal cellular component that defines and drives the disease known as السرطان البنكرياسي (pancreatic cancer), indicating that the presence of these atypical, cancerous cells is the hallmark of this specific malignancy.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 17 -- Level 1 -- Community 235
retrieved_docs.content: site‑specific benign tumors such as “ورم القضيب الحميد”, “ورم مبيضي حميد”, and “ورم مهبلي حميد” classified under “other and unspecified benign neoplasm”; “ورم حميد” is an “isa” relationship to “ورم غدي عضلي”. Malignant neoplasms include “cancer of other female genital organs”, “cancer of rectum and anus”, “cancer; other respiratory and intrathoracic” (e.g., squamous cell carcinoma of the trachea, which is also a possible finding of the symptom “سُعالٌ”/cough), “سرطان المعدة” (gastric cancer), and “ورم خبيث في الجلد، آخر” (other non‑epithelial skin cancer). Follow‑up after treatment of malignant neoplasms is captured as a child of the medical‑examination hierarchy. Respiratory diseases such as chronic bronchitis, bronchial dilatation, cystic fibrosis, and “bronchial dilatation” are coded alongside “non‑inflammatory conditions of the cervix uteri”. Immunological abnormalities are recorded as “abnormal immunological findings in serum”. Inflammatory and infectious conditions are organized

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2157
retrieved_docs.content: The pleura, the serous membrane that envelops the lungs, is classified as a component of the broader category of اضطراب جنبي (lateral disorder). This hierarchical relationship indicates that pleural conditions are considered within the spectrum of disorders affecting the lateral aspects of the thoracic cavity, underscoring the pleura’s relevance in the diagnosis and management of side‑related thoracic pathologies.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 17 -- Level 4 -- Community 2908
retrieved_docs.content: Malignant Cellular Infiltrate is a finding of disease and is categorized as cancer; other and unspecified primary, indicating that the presence of malignant cellular infiltrate reflects an underlying malignant neoplasm of unknown primary origin. Recognizing this finding is essential for guiding further diagnostic work‑up and management of patients with cancers where the primary site cannot be identified.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 9 -- Level 2 -- Community 1039
retrieved_docs.content: Primary malignant neoplasm of the head of the pancreas and primary malignant neoplasm of the thymus are both classified as primary malignant neoplastic proliferations, which are pathological processes. As malignant tumors arising in distinct anatomical sites—the pancreatic head and the thymus—each represents an uncontrolled growth of neoplastic cells that characterizes a serious pathological condition. Recognizing these neoplasms as primary malignant neoplastic proliferations emphasizes their role as aggressive cancerous processes that require prompt diagnostic evaluation and targeted oncologic management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 14 -- Level 5 -- Community 2926
retrieved_docs.content: Allowed qualifier is recorded as a diagnostic label for بيلة سِّيسْتينِية, عضال غدي, and متلازمة الضائقة الجنينية, indicating that this qualifier is applied when identifying these distinct medical conditions. بيلة سِّيسْتينِية refers to a hereditary disorder affecting the respiratory and digestive systems, عضال غدي denotes a glandular tumor, and متلازمة الضائقة الجنينية describes a syndrome of fetal distress. Recognizing Allowed qualifier as the diagnosis for each of these entities helps clinicians categorize and manage the respective diseases appropriately.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 19 -- Level 4 -- Community 2902
retrieved_docs.content: Malignant tumor cells are abnormal cells that characterize cancers classified as other and unspecified primary, indicating that these atypical cells constitute the pathological basis of such unspecified primary malignancies.

Together, these relationships demonstrate how the classification integrates diverse malignant neoplasms, connects specific tumor subtypes to broader malignant categories, identifies anatomical sites of disease extension, and relates morphological descriptors and clinical findings across organ systems, which is essential for accurate diagnosis, coding, and patient management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 9 -- Level 1 -- Community 93
retrieved_docs.content: abnormalities, treatment‑related complications, and a wide array of multilingual disease entities into a comprehensive classification system that supports accurate diagnosis, coding, reporting, and patient management across diverse medical domains.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 8 -- Level 1 -- Community 88
retrieved_docs.content: further integrate into the maternal antenatal complication spectrum. Gynecologic neoplasms of the female reproductive tract, specifically cervical tumor and vaginal tumor, are also identified as independent risk factors that elevate pregnancy to a high‑risk status, necessitating intensive monitoring and specialized management to protect maternal and fetal health. Collectively, these placental, coagulation, uterine, and reproductive‑system abnormalities converge on the central clinical entities of high‑risk pregnancy and bleeding in pregnancy, guiding diagnosis, risk stratification, and comprehensive obstetric hemorrhage management.
